title: Number of one-year-olds who are not vaccinated against measles
caption:
Number of one-year-olds who are not vaccinated against measles, 2023

The map displays the absolute count of unvaccinated children across the globe using a color scale ranging from light beige for zero cases to deep maroon for one million cases. The visualization reveals a distinct geographic concentration of high numbers in populous nations across Asia and Africa, while North America, Europe, and Australia generally appear in lighter shades indicating lower absolute totals. India and Nigeria occupy the highest category on the scale, with the map showing both countries in the darkest red band representing one million unvaccinated one-year-olds. Pakistan, Indonesia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Ethiopia also fall within this uppermost range, displaying the same deep maroon color. China and Brazil appear in the second-darkest band, corresponding to the 300,000 to 1 million range. A middle tier of orange shades covers countries such as Mexico, the Philippines, and South Africa, placing them in the 30,000 to 100,000 bracket. The United States, Canada, and most of Western Europe sit in the lightest bands, indicating fewer than 10,000 unvaccinated children in those territories. Greenland and several island nations are marked with diagonal hatching to signify no available data. The distribution shows that the largest absolute numbers of unprotected children cluster in specific high-population regions rather than spreading evenly across continents. Measles is a highly contagious viral disease, most common in young children, and its effects include blindness, inflammation of the brain, severe diarrhea, and severe respiratory infections such as pneumonia. The data shows that the highest burdens of unvaccinated children are visible in South Asia and sub-Saharan Africa.
